title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Browser and Node Client Systems · Mastery Check
summary: A cross-runtime release reviewer evaluating a changed provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for deterministic tests, fault injection, contract evidence, and observability, producing fault-injected client verification harness. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch fault-injected client verification harness. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Test request construction and response handling with an injected deterministic transport and sanitized fixtures. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Use a local test server for protocol integration while controlling redirects, delays, truncation, TLS, and cleanup. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Verify documented provider and consumer contracts against changed schemas, status codes, and version boundaries. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Fake Transport Fixtures · changed case · Quiz Fake Transport Fixtures
steps.instruction:
quiz case — universal Fetch release candidate test T-2 injects a delayed header, truncated gzip body, malformed JSON, cross-origin redirect, and cancellation. Assert the outgoing request, cleanup, failure taxonomy, attempt count, low-cardinality telemetry, and no secret leakage. Competency probe: Test request construction and response handling with an injected deterministic transport and sanitized fixtures. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Competency probe: Record stable client duration, method, route or safe target, status, error type, attempts, bytes, and trace context without high-cardinality secrets. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

For Fetch and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Http Telemetry, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Logging the complete URL and headers produces ideal HTTP observability. This remains false for TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch.
